title: Add right-stick volume bumps
summary: 0.6.66 adds one-step right-stick up/down volume bumps in the CUA input lane while leaving the current horizontal image/video transport behavior alone.
changes: The CUA input module now treats right-stick vertical excursions as one-shot volume bumps when trigger is not held.
Volume bumps reuse the existing absolute player volume setter instead of adding a new transport or plugin action surface.
Horizontal image stepping, horizontal video skip/scrub, slideshow timing, and sync behavior are unchanged in this slice.
reasoning: The vertical right-stick lane was currently unused once player focus was captured, which makes it a safe place to add volume control. This slice stays inside the CUA input module and preserves the existing axis-lock split so horizontal transport and vertical volume remain separate.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: In CUA focus mode, nudging the right stick up raises volume and nudging it down lowers volume.
Each vertical excursion applies a single bump and then waits for recenter or direction change before firing again.
knownIssues: This slice does not revisit the default-preset autoload seam.
This slice does not change movie transport, image slideshow behavior, sync cadence, or trigger-modified input behavior.
verification: FASyncRuntime.CuaPlayerInputModule.cs now handles right-stick vertical excursions through the existing player volume setter.
No scene-runtime transport or sync files changed for this slice.
